title: Leak-Safe Hierarchical Categorical Expert
summary: Driver, Race, Race_Year, and compound interactions are high-cardinality and likely carry strong priors, but naive encodings can leak badly in repeated sequential rows.
rationale: Driver, Race, Race_Year, and compound interactions are high-cardinality and likely carry strong priors, but naive encodings can leak badly in repeated sequential rows. Ordered boosting and internal cross-fitting were designed exactly for this problem and are more principled than plain frequency features.
implementation_hint: Test either CatBoost with ordered boosting or explicit cross-fitted target encodings for hierarchical keys such as Driver, Race, Race_Year, Driver_Compound, Driver_Race, and Race_Compound. Validate with grouped chronological folds plus a small embargo around held-out race segments so repeated near-duplicate states do not leak through encodings.
expected_effect: Can recover signal from team/driver/circuit habits that current frequency features leave on the table, while reducing optimistic CV from category leakage.
risk: If folds are not strictly grouped and causal, CV may look better than leaderboard reality; if grouped too harshly, the expert may underfit rare drivers.
